context.architecture: Clean Architecture - Handler → Domain Service → Infrastructure
context.database: PostgreSQL with sqlc (type-safe SQL → Go code generation)
context.storage: Cloudflare R2 (hot) → AWS S3 Glacier (cold archive)
context.auth: JWT tokens with Cloudflare auth token embedded in claims

tasks.title: Add webhook signature verification
tasks.description: Cloudflare webhook endpoint has NO signature verification. Anyone can POST fake recording.ready events and inject malicious data into our system.
fix_steps: 1. Read the raw request body BEFORE parsing JSON
2. Get X-Cloudflare-Signature header from request
3. Verify signature using HMAC-SHA256 with webhook secret
4. Only parse JSON if signature is valid
5. Return 401 Unauthorized if signature missing or invalid
tasks.impact: CRITICAL SECURITY VULNERABILITY - attackers can inject fake recordings

tasks.title: Add recording access audit logs
tasks.description: No audit log created when recordings are downloaded. GDPR requires tracking who accessed personal data (video recordings contain faces/voices).
fix_steps: 1. Add actorID and ipAddress parameters to GetDownloadURL function
2. After getting recording but before returning URL, call CreateAuditLog
3. Include recording metadata in audit log (size, duration, room_id)
4. Update handler to pass actor info from JWT claims and request IP
requires: Handler must extract actor_id from JWT claims
Handler must get client IP from c.ClientIP()
tasks.impact: GDPR violation - no forensics if data leak occurs

tasks.title: Verify recording hard delete
tasks.description: DeleteRecording calls MarkRecordingDeleted which might be soft delete (sets deleted_at). GDPR requires actual data removal from storage AND database.
fix_steps: 1. Check apps/api/db/queries/recordings.sql for MarkRecordingDeleted SQL
2. If it's UPDATE (soft delete), change to DELETE (hard delete)
3. OR rename existing query and add new DeleteRecording query
4. Run sqlc generate to regenerate Go code
5. Update service to use hard delete
tasks.expected_sql_if_wrong: UPDATE recordings SET status = 'deleted' WHERE id = $1
tasks.note: Storage deletion is already implemented on line 282 - just need DB hard delete
tasks.impact: GDPR violation - data not actually deleted on request

tasks.title: Add tenant room limit validation
tasks.description: CreateRoom does not check if tenant has reached max_concurrent_rooms quota. Tenants can create unlimited rooms bypassing their quota.
fix_steps: 1. Add db *db.Queries to Service struct (already there)
2. At start of CreateRoom, get tenant: s.db.GetTenant(ctx, input.TenantID)
3. Count active rooms: s.db.CountActiveRoomsByTenant(ctx, input.TenantID)
4. Compare: if activeCount >= tenant.MaxConcurrentRooms, return error
5. Only then proceed with Cloudflare API call
requires: GetTenant query exists in db package
tasks.impact: Tenants can create unlimited rooms, bypass quota

tasks.title: Add duplicate participant check
tasks.description: JoinRoom does not check if external_user_id already exists in room. Same user can join multiple times creating duplicate entries.
fix_steps: 1. After room validation, check for existing participant
2. Use GetParticipantByExternalUserAndRoom query (check if exists)
3. If exists AND LeftAt is NULL (still active), handle duplicate
4. DECISION: Return existing participant with new token (allow multi-device) OR return error (block)
5. Recommended: Allow multi-device by returning existing with refreshed token
tasks.impact: Duplicate participants, incorrect counts, wasted Cloudflare resources

tasks.title: Add participant room capacity check
tasks.description: JoinRoom does not check if room has reached max_participants_per_room limit. Rooms can exceed capacity.
tasks.current_code: // NO CAPACITY CHECK - can add unlimited participants
fix_steps: 1. After room validation, get tenant config
2. Count active participants: CountActiveParticipantsByRoom
3. Compare against tenant.MaxParticipantsPerRoom
4. Return ErrRoomFull if exceeded
tasks.impact: Rooms exceed capacity, Cloudflare might reject, poor UX

tasks.title: Add WebSocket broadcast for participant events
tasks.description: No WebSocket events broadcasted when participants join or leave. Other participants' UIs don't update in real-time.
tasks.current_code: // After creating participant, no broadcast to other participants
fix_steps: 1. Import encoding/json at top of file
2. After participant created in JoinRoom, create message struct
3. Marshal to JSON and broadcast via hub.BroadcastToRoom
4. Same for LeaveRoom - broadcast participant.left event
5. Exclude the joining participant from receiving their own join event
tasks.impact: UIs don't show real-time participant updates

tasks.title: Add recording webhook timeout fallback
tasks.description: If Cloudflare webhook never arrives, recording stuck in 'processing' status forever. No timeout, no retry, no fallback polling.
fix_steps: 1. Create new file: apps/api/internal/infrastructure/jobs/recording_check.go
2. Create CheckStalledRecordings function
3. Query for recordings WHERE status='processing' AND updated_at < NOW() - 1 hour
4. For each stalled recording, poll Cloudflare API for actual status
5. If Cloudflare says 'ready', process webhook manually
6. If Cloudflare says 'failed', mark as failed in DB
7. Integrate with lifecycle manager (run every 30 min)
8. Add to main.go lifecycle manager start
requires: Need to add MarkRecordingFailed SQL query if not exists
Add to lifecycle manager in main.go
tasks.impact: Recordings lost forever if webhook fails

tasks.title: Add active recording check before starting
tasks.description: StartRecording does not check if recording already in progress for room. Can start multiple concurrent recordings.
fix_steps: 1. Before calling Cloudflare, check for existing active recording
2. Use GetActiveRecordingByRoom query
3. If found, return error 'recording already in progress'

tasks.title: Rollback Cloudflare on DB failure in CreateRoom
tasks.description: In CreateRoom, if Cloudflare succeeds but DB insert fails, Cloudflare meeting is orphaned (created but never tracked).
fix_steps: 1. After successful Cloudflare call, if DB fails, cleanup Cloudflare
2. Call cfClient.EndMeeting() to delete orphaned meeting

tasks.title: Add WebSocket broadcast for room events
tasks.description: No WebSocket events for room created/ended. Tenant dashboards don't update in real-time.
fix_steps: 1. In CreateRoom, after DB insert, broadcast 'room.created' to tenant
2. In EndRoom, before return, broadcast 'room.ended' to room participants

tasks.title: Add tenant configuration system
tasks.description: No centralized tenant configuration for: force_recording, duplicate_participant_policy, empty_room_timeout, recording_retention_days, etc.
fix_steps: 1. Add tenant_config JSONB column to tenants table
2. Create migration file
3. Run migration
4. Update services to read config from tenant.Config

tasks.title: Add metadata column to rooms/participants/recordings
tasks.description: Consumer backend IDs (tuition_highway_class_id) stored in config field. Should be separate metadata field for backend integration data.

tasks.title: Add bulk participant add API
tasks.description: For all-hands meetings with 150+ people, 150 sequential API calls is too slow. Need batch endpoint.
fix_steps: 1. Add new handler: BulkAdd(c *gin.Context)
2. Parse array of participant requests
3. Use database transaction for atomicity
4. Loop through and add each participant
5. Collect results (success + failures)
6. Return array of participant IDs + tokens
7. Add route: POST /api/v1/rooms/:id/participants/bulk

tasks.title: Add empty room auto-cleanup
tasks.description: Rooms stay active forever if no one joins. Wastes tenant quota.
fix_steps: 1. Create background job running every 10 minutes
2. Query for active rooms with 0 participants for > 30 min
3. Auto-end each room
4. Create audit log entry
5. Make timeout configurable per tenant

tasks.title: Add force recording tenant config
tasks.description: Tuition Highway needs ALL classes recorded automatically, tutors can't disable. Need tenant-level enforcement.
fix_steps: 1. Add force_recording to tenant config
2. In JoinRoom, check if joining participant is first host
3. If first host AND tenant.force_recording = true, auto-start recording
4. Frontend should hide recording toggle when force_recording enabled

tasks.title: Add docker-compose.yml for local development
tasks.description: No docker-compose exists. Need 1-command local dev setup with all services.
tasks.fix_code:
services:
  api:
    build: ./apps/api
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    environment:
      - DATABASE_URL=postgres://chalk:chalk@postgres:5432/chalk?sslmode=disable
      - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379
      - ENV=development
    depends_on:
      - postgres
      - redis

  whiteboard:
    image: excalidraw/excalidraw-room
    ports:
      - "3002:80"
    environment:
      - PORT=80

  postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=chalk
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=chalk
      - POSTGRES_DB=chalk
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./apps/api/db/migrations:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data

volumes:
  postgres_data:
  redis_data:
fix_steps: 1. Create docker-compose.yml at repo root
2. Add api service with build context
3. Add excalidraw-room service (pre-built image)
4. Add postgres + redis dependencies
5. Mount migrations for auto-init
6. Test with: docker-compose up

tasks.title: Add Excalidraw whiteboard ECS service
tasks.description: Deploy excalidraw-room as separate ECS service for production whiteboard collab.

context.current_infra: ECS on EC2 (not Fargate), single cluster, ASG managed
context.pattern: Copy service.tf pattern, add new task definition + service
context.alb: Add path-based routing: /whiteboard/* → whiteboard target group

fix_steps: 1. Create infrastructure/terraform/modules/ecr/whiteboard.tf - 2nd ECR repo
2. Create infrastructure/terraform/modules/ecs/service_whiteboard.tf:
- aws_ecs_task_definition.whiteboard (image: excalidraw/excalidraw-room)
- aws_ecs_service.whiteboard
- aws_lb_target_group.whiteboard (port 80, health: /)
- aws_lb_listener_rule.whiteboard (path: /whiteboard/*)
- aws_security_group.whiteboard_tasks
3. Add variables: whiteboard_task_cpu (256), whiteboard_task_memory (512)
4. Update environments/dev/main.tf - enable whiteboard service
5. Update environments/prod/main.tf - enable whiteboard service
6. terraform plan → terraform apply

easy_wins_for_beginner: P0-003 - Just check SQL and maybe change UPDATE to DELETE (15 min)
P0-004 - Add 10 lines before Cloudflare call (15 min)
P0-006 - Very similar to P0-004, copy pattern (15 min)
P1-001 - Add 3 lines at start of function (10 min)
needs_decision: P0-005 - Allow multi-device (return existing) or block duplicates (return error)?
architecture_notes: Services use interfaces - easy to mock for testing
All services receive *db.Queries - can call any SQL query
WebSocket hub is optional (check if s.hub != nil)
Redis room state is optional (check if s.roomState != nil)
Error wrapping: use fmt.Errorf('context: %w', err) for stack traces
